page: OVHcloud r3-16 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3074.87 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1914.0. Text processing capabilities are also strong. However, multi-core performance is average to weak, as shown by a Geekbench multi-core score of 3414.0 and a passmark CPU score of 5422.13. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, reaching 66.32 GB/sec for read operations and 41.46 GB/sec for write operations, though small-block cached read bandwidth is relatively weak.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio but limited overall core count, the r3-16 is suited for memory-sensitive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. Appropriate workloads include small-scale databases, caching layers, text processing pipelines, and lightweight single-threaded applications. It is less suitable for highly parallelized tasks, heavy compilation, or high-throughput static web serving due to its lower multi-core and network throughput benchmarks.
description: OVHcloud r3-16 is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Milan processor clocked at 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core, alongside a 50 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU and text processing performance in benchmarks, though its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities are more modest. The r3-16 represents a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand extensive parallel processing. It is well-suited for hosting small-scale databases, caching systems, text processing applications, and single-threaded utility services.
